commit.message:
Fix pivoting-agent lint: put search_skills/list_skills on same line as "do not"

The lint test checks per-line for search_skills()/list_skills() references
and only exempts lines containing "do not" or "never call". The prohibition
was split across two lines, so the line with the function names didn't match.

commit.message:
Add pivot-aware scanning to network-recon, improve dashboard agent lifecycle

network-recon skill:
- Add "Pivot Mode" section with two-phase scanning approach
- Phase 1: fast host discovery on pivot host (ARP, ping sweep, DNS zone,
  single-port proxychains sweep)
- Phase 2: targeted nmap through proxychains on known-live hosts only
- Document nmap MCP server limitation (no proxychains support)
- Add static nmap alternative for speed-critical scenarios
- Add pivot-specific troubleshooting entries

Dashboard (tail-agent.py):
- Auto-discover new agents from tasks directory (no .dashboard file needed)
- Red *stopped* indicator in pane header for dead agents
- Detect agent completion via JSONL final message + 5s mtime debounce
- Detect killed agents (TaskStop) via 30s mtime staleness
- Keep stopped agents visible instead of auto-removing
- Space bar toggles agents in/out from browser overlay

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add scaled exit strategy and Moralis fallback for Bitquery 402 errors

- Replace single +50% TP with 4-tranche scaled exits: 2x→25%, 4x→25%, 8x→25%, 16x→25%
- Stop loss at -30% still exits full remaining position
- Each partial sell logged to activity log (type: partial_sell)
- New trade fields: remainingTokens, tranchesSold, realizedPnl, partialSells[]
- Add Bq402Error class; scoreWallet falls back to Moralis API on 402
- moralisScoreWallet uses Moralis /swaps endpoint for Solana wallet history
- scanner.js passes moralisKey from config to scoreWallet
- Frontend: Tranches column in open/closed positions tables
- Frontend: scaled exit description replaces TP% input
- Frontend: partial_sell activity type with 📈 icon
- Frontend: moralisKey synced to backend on save and initial connect

commit.message:
Add MACHIterative: error-correction inner loop meta-learner

Iterates K times on demos (write → test → observe error → adjust)
instead of one-shot demo processing. Error features (loss, accuracy,
confidence, step index) are detached; gradient flows through writes.

New files:
- models/error_observation.py: ErrorProjection (4 features → d_meta)

New classes in universal_module.py:
- MACHIterative: K-step inner loop with GRU + transformer + patches
- IterativePatchedModel: Qwen wrapper with patch hooks (no modulation)

Training support:
- run_episode_iterative, meta_train_iterative in two_channel_train.py
- --iterative and --n-inner-steps flags in run_two_channel.py

~760K params, simpler than TwoChannel (~1.25M). Run with:
  python scripts/run_two_channel.py --task continuous_linear --iterative

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add --reading-rhythm for text-aware audiobook pacing

Hybrid approach: text analysis scores each detected TTS pause based on
punctuation type (period 450ms > semicolon 250ms > comma 150ms > dash
180ms), then extends only existing silent regions — no forced splices,
no TTS markers, no artifacts.

Features: glue-word protection (EN+FR), punctuation-to-audio gap
matching with deduplication, 70ms fallback for unmatched significant
gaps, 1.5s aggregate cap per sentence, 1.15x multiplier for French
syllable-timed rhythm. Reviewed by audiobook-reader expert agent.

commit.message:
Fix Fantasy always starting at Spieltag 1 (ClubProvider race condition)

Root cause: ClubProvider loads async, so clubId is '' initially. The
useActiveGameweek query is disabled (enabled: !!clubId) and reports
isLoading: false for disabled queries. The loading guard only checked
(!!clubId && activeGwLoading), which was false when clubId was empty.
Result: currentGw fell through to fallback value 1.

Fix: Also check clubLoading from ClubProvider in the skeleton guard,
so we wait for the club to resolve before rendering GW-dependent content.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
chore: update nopal submodule to v0.2.0

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: update dependencies and frontend platform conflicts

* fixed failing build on web

* fix: replace invalid Tailwind opacity patterns with solid colors

* fixed ios build issues and refactored every file into native and web specific version

* fix: point .tsx fallback re-exports to .web instead of .native

The .tsx file is the bundler fallback — if platform-specific resolution
fails, it loads this file. Re-exporting from .native pulled in native-only
deps (react-native-maps, datetimepicker) which crash on web. Changed all
fallbacks (Map, ThemeSelector, BirthdatePicker, AuthInput, Card,
PrimaryButton) to re-export from their .web variants instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: restore actual image imports in auth screen

Replaced placeholder.com URLs with require() calls to the actual
Swami Chinmayananda images in assets/images/landing/. The ImageCarousel
component handles both string and module sources via resolveImageSrc().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: restore PrimaryButton NativeWind classes and web button element

Native: restore NativeWind className support with theme tokens
(bg-primary, text-backgroundStrong) instead of hardcoded hex values.
Web: restore HTML <button> element with type="button" to prevent form
submission, using Tailwind classes matching the original implementation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: restore dark mode support in Card.native.tsx

Replaced hardcoded white background and gray border with NativeWind
theme classes (bg-card dark:bg-card-dark, border-borderColor
dark:border-borderColor-dark) matching the web variant's approach.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: restore platform-specific behavior in BirthdatePicker.native.tsx

Restored Android auto-close on selection, iOS-only Done button, and
platform-appropriate display mode (spinner on iOS, default on Android).
Also restored NativeWind classes and dark mode support instead of
hardcoded inline styles.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: restore Verdana font for password fields in AuthInput.native.tsx

The original used Verdana for secure text fields with content, which
provides more visible password dots on native platforms. Restored the
conditional font family logic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: complete opacity pattern migration and remove dead code

- Remove commented-out LeafletView import in index.web.tsx
- Migrate remaining border-muted/20 patterns to border-stone-200/700
  in settings layout and settings page for consistency
- Migrate remaining bg-primary/50 to bg-orange-300 in onboarding
  steps 3, 4, 5 (matching steps 1 and 2)
- Migrate bg-muted/50 to bg-stone-100 in AuthInput variants

* fix: upgrade react-native-reanimated to ~4.1.1 for RN 0.81 compat

v3.16.x is incompatible with React Native 0.81+ causing the
folly/coro/Coroutine.h build error on iOS. Upgraded to ~4.1.1
which is the version expected by Expo SDK 54.

commit.message:
Add delete confirmation for recurring events: this only vs this & future

When deleting a recurring event (part of a series), shows a dialog with
three options: "This Event Only", "This & Future Events", or Cancel.
"This & future" deletes the selected event and all later events in the
same series. Non-recurring events still delete immediately without a
dialog. Uses optimistic updates with rollback on failure.

commit.message:
Add cloud-hypervisor sandbox microVM infrastructure (Gate 2)

- Create NixOS guest config for sandbox microVMs (nix/ch/sandbox-vm.nix)
  with cloud-hypervisor, TAP networking, virtiofs shares for bin/data/creds
- Add choiros-ch-sandbox-live and choiros-ch-sandbox-dev to flake
- Add br-choiros bridge (10.0.0.0/24), NAT, trusted firewall on host
- Add cloud-hypervisor, virtiofsd, socat, iproute2 to host packages
- Replace static sandbox-live/sandbox-dev systemd services with
  VM-managed approach via ovh-runtime-ctl.sh
- Implement ovh-runtime-ctl.sh: TAP creation, VM lifecycle,
  socat port forwarding, health check polling

Architecture: hypervisor calls runtime-ctl ensure/stop → runtime-ctl
manages cloud-hypervisor VM + socat forwarder → sandbox runs inside
VM on bridge network → socat forwards 127.0.0.1:PORT to VM IP.

commit.message:
Fix blocked deployments — restore sass.logError so build failures don't halt pipeline

process.exit(1) was stopping the entire workflow on SCSS errors, meaning no files
were being deployed to the server at all. Switching back to sass.logError so that:
- If build succeeds: compiled app.css is deployed
- If build fails: error is logged, committed app.css (with !important fix) is deployed
Either way SFTP runs and the correct CSS reaches the server.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: SE 連打時の無音を修正（new Audio() 方式に変更）

iOS Safari では同一 <audio> 要素の pause()/play() 連打がブロックされる。
毎回 new Audio(src) を生成する方式に変更することで
click ハンドラ内のユーザージェスチャー判定を維持しつつ即時再生を実現。
元の <audio> 要素は src 取得用として引き続き保持。

commit.message:
Full quality redesign: better models, Gaussian smoothing, preview clips, UX upgrades

- Upgrade detector to YOLOv8x and ReID to osnet_x1_0 for better accuracy
- Replace Kalman+EMA+velocity-clamp renderer with bidirectional Gaussian
  smoothing (scipy gaussian_filter1d) — zero-lag, no breathing/jumps
- Add hardware-accelerated H.264 encoding (h264_videotoolbox on macOS)
- Add preview clips: 5s bbox-overlay clip before full render for verification
- Add timeline visualization bars in DancerGrid showing per-track coverage
- Add drag-and-drop fragment reassignment in FragmentPanel
- Add ETA progress tracking during analysis and generation
- Confidence-weighted ReID embeddings (30 samples instead of 10)
- Delete kalman_predictor.py (no longer needed)

commit.message:
refactor: split monolithic tracker into Issue and Workflow concerns

Separates the single tracker hash into two distinct data structures:
- Tracker::Issue: issue-level data (classification, branch, ports, worktrees)
  persisted at .issues/{n}/issue.yaml
- Tracker::Workflow: per-run data (adw_id, status, plan_path, phase_comments)
  persisted at .issues/{n}/workflows/{adw_id}.yaml

This eliminates the polymorphic _type: :patch hack and the dual-tracker
pattern (main_tracker + patch_tracker), replacing them with a clean
separation where issue_tracker flows through unchanged while workflow
trackers are created fresh per run. Includes legacy migration support
and backward-compat delegations on Tracker class methods.

commit.message:
Redesign app API: global state, richer callbacks, remove events.h

- rc_app is now a file-scope static in app_glfw.c; all public functions
  take no rc_app* parameter; rc_app_make -> rc_app_init (void return)
- rc_app_callbacks struct groups all callbacks + ctx pointer
- rc_app_swap removed; rc_app_request_render sets viewport, calls
  on_render, and swaps — consistent with the window-refresh path
- keys.h: replaced rc_key/rc_action with rc_scancode (full keypad);
  on_key_down/up receive physical scancode, on_key_char receives
  codepoint + mods at OS auto-repeat cadence
- Added: on_focus_gained/lost (clears current_mods on loss),
  on_mouse_enter/leave, on_mouse_move, on_mouse_wheel, on_minimize,
  on_maximize, on_update(dt), on_render
- events.h deleted; parameters passed directly to callbacks
- GLFW_INCLUDE_NONE removed (redundant: glad defines __gl_h_ etc.)
- test_app.c uses designated initializers and compound literal for desc

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(core): resolve IPC dedup lock and autonomous init race condition

- trinity_ipc.py: Replace dead `asyncio.Lock() if False` with lazy
  `_get_seen_lock()` factory for proper dedup mutex initialization
- ouroboros/integration.py: Add `_get_autonomous_init_lock()` to guard
  check-then-set of `_autonomous_initialized` in both init and shutdown,
  preventing TOCTOU race where concurrent coroutines double-initialize
